cards.prompt: An iterator method is lazy: calling it returns an object without running the body, so the caller's line prints before the iterator body starts.
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; IEnumerable<int> Produce(){ Console.WriteLine("Iterator: start."); yield return 1; Console.WriteLine("Iterator: after 1"); } var seq = Produce(); Console.WriteLine("Caller: got the object, nothing ran yet."); foreach (var i in seq) Console.WriteLine($"Caller: {i}");
cards.expectedOutput:
Caller: got the object, nothing ran yet.
Iterator: start.
Caller: 1
Iterator: after 1

cards.prompt: Two iterator objects are created but no body runs on call: the in-body counter is still 0.
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; int calls=0; IEnumerable<int> Nums(){ calls++; yield return 1; yield return 2; } var a = Nums(); var b = Nums(); Console.WriteLine($"created 2 iterators, bodies run: {calls}");

cards.prompt: foreach desugars into GetEnumerator() + while(MoveNext()){ Current }; each MoveNext resumes the body to the next yield return.
cards.code: using System; using System.Collections.Generic; using System.Text; IEnumerable<int> Nums(){ yield return 10; yield return 20; yield return 30; } var e = Nums().GetEnumerator(); var sb = new StringBuilder(); while (e.MoveNext()) sb.Append(e.Current).Append(" "); Console.WriteLine(sb.ToString().Trim());
